name: aml-anti-money-laundering
description: Anti-Money Laundering (AML) dataset modeling structuring/smurfing and layering typologies. Includes branches, KYC documents, device fingerprints, beneficiaries, wire transfers, and SAR filings. All enum distributions cited from FinCEN, BSA/IRS, FATF, and industry sources.

options._citation: US Census Bureau regional population shares 2024 (rounded)
FFIEC Branch Office Survey 2024: full-service ~62% of bank offices
FATF 2024 jurisdiction risk list + US bank customer geography (FDIC ~70% domestic)
BLS 2024 occupation distribution; cash_intensive_business ~2% per FinCEN typology guidance
Industry KYC pass rates: ~88% verified on first pass (LexisNexis 2024)
AML industry: ~78% low-risk, ~5% high-risk per AML risk-rating frameworks
FinCEN Customer Identification Program guidance + Persona ID-doc mix 2024
FDIC: ~78% of US bank customers use US-issued ID
Persona / Jumio 2024 verification pass rate ~86% auto-pass
StatCounter US 2024: iOS ~32%, Android ~28%, Win desktop ~18% in banking sessions
FDIC Statistics on Depository Institutions 2024: checking ~44% of retail deposit accounts; offshore ~2% per FinCEN typology
BIS Triennial Survey 2022 adjusted for US-based bank customer mix
FFIEC Call Report 2024: ~86% accounts active, ~8% dormant (no activity 12+ months)
Fed cross-border wire stats 2024 + FATF high-risk corridors
ACH Originator survey 2023 (NACHA): family + business ~60% of declared payees
Fed Payments Study 2024: ACH 34%, card 32%, cash deposits ~12% by count at retail branches
Bank-of-US deposit currency mix; non-USD <12% retail deposits
FinCEN FY2024: ~5% of accounts subject of SAR; structuring dominates SAR volume (~62% per FinCEN typology mix)
ABA 2024 Consumer Survey: mobile ~30%, online ~24% as primary channel
Fed Wire Statistics 2024: FEDWIRE 42% of US wire volume by count, SWIFT for cross-border
BIS Triennial 2022: USD ~78% of cross-border wire denomination
SWIFT MT103 purpose code distribution 2023 + Fed cross-border survey
Fed Wire ops 2024: ~88% straight-through processing, ~3% subject to review
FinCEN SAR statistics FY2024: initial filings ~62%, continuing ~26%
FinCEN FY2024 SAR by activity: structuring ~42%, ML ~22%, fraud ~18%

options.pattern: Customer {{number}} flagged for review of activity pattern.

options._citation: FinCEN is sole filing destination; OFAC/FBI referrals secondary
Industry: ~4% of SARs result in LE referral (FinCEN annual report)

_gap_classification._description: Gaps in this dataset, classified by who owns the fix. Engine and assess gaps affect every RealityDB template. Template gaps are specific to this pack. Command prerequisites are reminders for the human running the CLI.

engine_limitations.consequence: transaction_class=structuring_suspect can have any amount, not just $9,000-$9,999
wire_transfers have valid FKs but no sequenced 3+ hop chains

engine_limitations.affects: shared device fingerprints, document numbers, IPs across customers
engine_limitations.consequence: Network-graph queries (shared device across 'unrelated' customers) return empty

engine_limitations.affects: all status columns and their related timestamps
engine_limitations.consequence: A 'closed' account may have null closed_at; a 'cancelled' wire may still have settled_at
--seed flag is accepted but not actually used; Math.random() is global. Same command produces different output each run.

assess_limitations.consequence: Assess reports '0/0 healthy' — does not detect FK relationships in SQL output, so Moat 4 is unverified by tooling
assess_limitations.fix_in: apps/cli/src/commands/assess.ts (add SQL FK parser, measure parent:child ratios)

assess_limitations.consequence: Assess does not check that status columns are consistent with their related timestamps
Assess pairs columns by name (e.g., onboarded_at vs created_at) and may flag valid orderings as failures depending on column position
assess_limitations.fix_in: apps/cli/src/commands/assess.ts (use schema metadata for explicit dependsOn pairs)

command_prerequisites: CLI must be v2.37.7+ (verify: realitydb --version OR node apps/cli/dist/index.js --version)
Pack must be studio-export format (run: comply doctor --fix to convert from studio-v4)
Use --seed for nominal reproducibility (note: P1 engine gap means this is currently advisory)
Use --format sql for watermark + assessment (JSON format does not get _realitydb_meta)
After ANY engine code change: full turbo cache clear before rebuild (rm -rf $env:LOCALAPPDATA\turbo)
Use 5K+ rows for any enum with weight <2% to ensure statistical visibility
what_this_dataset_does_well: FK integrity: all FKs reference real parent rows (Moat 1)
Temporal columns use past_date / future_date with realistic ranges (Moat 2 partial)
Cardinality: row counts deliberately non-flat in the schema's intent, though current engine flattens at generation (assess can't measure this either, see assess_limitations)
All 27 enums have research-backed weights with FinCEN/BSA/FATF/Fed/BIS citations (no UNIFORM)
9 tables with rich relational structure: customers, accounts, branches, KYC docs, devices, beneficiaries, transactions, wires, SARs
Smoke-test forbidden-pattern check passes for all 8+ legacy patterns AND new val_X_Y catcher (after engine patch)
